name: Flamel Legend
description: The Flamel Legend centers on Nicolas Flamel, a 14th-century Parisian scribe and bookseller, who, according to alchemical lore, discovered the secret of transmuting base metals into gold and achieving immortality through the Philosopher's Stone. The legend originated centuries after Flamel's death, primarily in 17th-century alchemical texts, and gained further traction in popular culture. Proponents point to supposed cryptic symbols and alchemical treatises attributed to Flamel as evidence of his success. These texts are interpreted as containing coded instructions for achieving the Magnum Opus. The legend often includes claims of Flamel and his wife Pernelle achieving immortality and secretly living on. Mainstream scholarship generally dismisses the Flamel Legend as a fabrication or a heavily embellished account. Historians note the lack of contemporary evidence supporting Flamel's alchemical pursuits and attribute the legend to the proliferation of alchemical literature during the Renaissance and early modern periods. The alchemical texts attributed to Flamel are now considered pseudepigrapha, likely written by later authors seeking to legitimize their own alchemical theories by associating them with a historical figure. The enduring appeal of the Flamel Legend lies in its promise of wealth, longevity, and esoteric knowledge, tapping into humanity's enduring fascination with alchemy and the search for the secrets of the universe.
subtitle: Alchemist, Philosopher's Stone, Immortality

claims.claim: Nicolas Flamel discovered the Philosopher's Stone.
claims.evidence: Alchemical texts attributed to Flamel containing cryptic symbols and instructions.
claims.criticism: Lack of contemporary evidence; texts likely pseudepigrapha written centuries later.

claims.claim: Flamel achieved transmutation of base metals into gold.
claims.evidence: Legends of Flamel's immense wealth despite his modest profession.
claims.criticism: Wealth is unsubstantiated by historical records; legend likely exaggerated.

claims.claim: Flamel and his wife Pernelle achieved immortality.
claims.evidence: Stories of their continued existence after their supposed deaths.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence; stories are based on speculation and unsubstantiated claims.

keyFigures.name: Nicolas Flamel
keyFigures.role: Central figure of the legend, alleged alchemist

keyFigures.name: Didier Kahn
keyFigures.role: Historian specializing in alchemy, critic of the Flamel legend

timeline.event: Publication of 'Livre des Figures Hiéroglyphiques' attributed to Flamel
Flamel Legend begins to solidify in alchemical literature

sources.author: Didier Kahn
